title: Bookkeeping client config
description: Configuration for one client of the reconciliation + payroll engine. One file per client. Real client files live in a private location (see clients/README.md); this schema documents the expected structure. Additional payroll-specific account fields are permitted beyond those listed here.

client_name.description: Exact legal/display name as it appears on statements.

canonical_name.description: UPPERCASE key used for client detection and reporting.

aliases.description: Other names the client appears as on statements (e.g. cardholder or DBA names). Matched case-insensitively.

bank_account.description: Human-readable label of the primary bank/GL account, e.g. "1010 · Checking #1234". May be empty.

bofa_account_number.description: Optional full account number used to disambiguate Bank of America statements.

company_code.description: Optional payroll provider company code (e.g. ADP) used to match payroll statements.

statement_types.description: Which parsers apply to this client's statements.

account_endings.description: Map a card/account last-4 to the account_type to use, e.g. {"3551": "chase_sapphire"}. Disambiguates statements whose product name only appears in a logo image (e.g. Chase Sapphire vs Ink) when a client holds multiple cards from the same bank.

cardholders.description: For multi-cardholder credit-card statements (e.g. AmEx): the list of cardholder names.

payroll_vendors.description: Keywords that route a transaction into the Payroll section.

cc_keywords.description: Keywords that identify credit-card payment transactions. May include the card’s last four digits to catch transfers to a specific card.

internal_transfer_keywords.description: AmEx credits matching any of these strings (case-insensitive) are treated as internal transfers between the client's own entities and listed individually rather than aggregated. Keeps counterparty names out of code.

bofa_credits_account.description: Optional Bank of America account ending (e.g. "1234") that marks a separate 'Payments and Other Credits' section in the statement. Omit for statements without such a section.

vendor_rules.description: Ordered rules that normalize raw transaction descriptions to clean vendor names. First matching rule wins.

contains.description: Substring that must appear in the description (case-insensitive).

starts_with.description: Description must start with this string (case-insensitive).

also_contains_any.description: At least one of these substrings must appear.

display_suffix.description: Optional suffix appended in parentheses, e.g. result becomes "Vendor (suffix)".

departments.description: Optional payroll department map: department code → { name, mode }. Drives how employees in that department are journaled.

mode.description: How this department is journaled, e.g. "admin_individual" or "coaches_rollup".

special_employees.description: Optional per-employee payroll overrides keyed by an employee/payee identifier. Each entry either names a single GL account or splits the amount across accounts. Add a `match` rule and `display_name` so the engine can identify and label the payee from config instead of hardcoded names in code.

match.description: How to identify this payee among parsed employees. Name matching ignores spaces.

dept.description: Department code the payee must be in, e.g. "001".

name_contains.description: Substring that must appear in the payee's name (spaces ignored).

display_name.description: Name to show on the journal rows for this payee.

placement.description: Whether this payee's rows are emitted before or after the regular per-individual admin block. Defaults to "after_admin".

account.description: Single GL account for this payee.

splits.description: Split the payee's amount across accounts. Use the literal string "remainder" for the last split's amount.

contractors_1099.description: ADP 1099 contractor definitions for payroll parsing. Each entry maps a contractor name pattern to a role used in journal routing.

name_contains.description: Substring of the contractor's name as it appears in the ADP PDF (spaces ignored).

skip_if_zero_net_pay.description: If true, omit this contractor from the journal entry when ADP reports $0.00 net pay (paid outside ADP that period).

contractor_display_name.description: Display name used in journal rows for the 1099 contractor split (if applicable).

check_payee_overrides.description: Manual check-number → payee name mappings used when OCR cannot extract a payee from a check image. Applies to all checking-account parsers (BofA, Citi, WF, BMO, USBank, etc.). Keys are check numbers as strings; values are the payee display name.

description_strip_suffixes.description: Strings stripped from the trailing end of raw transaction descriptions before vendor normalization. Applied in order; useful for removing location or cardholder noise appended by some banks. Case-insensitive.

statement_access_accounts.description: Optional notes on how statement access is obtained per source.

tracker_key.description: Canonical ALL_CAPS_UNDERSCORE key used in sheets_config.json and reconciliation_log.csv. When present, _normalize_client_key() uses this instead of the hardcoded fallback table in log_utils.py.

payroll_key.description: Short convenience key for `python payroll.py <key> <pdf>` dispatch (e.g. "acme"). When set alongside payroll_format, payroll.py resolves the format and config file from this client without a hardcoded table.

payroll_format.description: Which payroll journal format this client uses. When set, payroll.py can dispatch by client key without a hardcoded map entry.

features.description: Optional per-client feature flags. These drive client-specific parser/journal behavior from config instead of hardcoded client-name checks in the code. Unknown flags are permitted.

transaction_aggregations.description: Roll up many transactions matching a keyword into a single line (e.g. a recurring platform's many transfers). Keeps specific vendor/brand names in client config, never hardcoded in parser code. Applied by the BofA checking and AmEx checking parsers to both deposits and withdrawals.

match.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against the transaction description; every matching transaction is collapsed into one line.

label.description: Display name for the single aggregated line (used on checking statements).

card_label.description: Optional display name for the aggregated line on card statements (AmEx). Defaults to `label` when omitted.
